title: Gathering Your Focus
intention: To gather scattered attention into a calm, ready alertness before a stretch of work.

lines.text: Welcome. Take a moment to arrive here.
Sit upright, with your feet flat on the floor.
Let your hands rest where they are comfortable.
Keep your eyes soft, or close them. Whichever helps you settle.
Notice that you have stopped. There is nowhere else to be right now.
Take one slow breath in. And let it out fully.
Again. Breathe in. And release.
Your attention may feel scattered. That is fine. We will gather it now.
Feel the weight of your body in the chair.
Feel your feet pressing down into the floor.
Bring your awareness to the breath at your nose.
Notice the cool air coming in. The warm air going out.
When your mind wanders, simply notice, and come back to the breath.
There is nothing to chase. Just this breath, then the next.
Now picture the work in front of you. See it clearly.
Choose the one thing you will begin with.
Let everything else move into the background for now.
Take a fuller breath in, and feel your energy lift slightly.
Your mind is clearer now. Steady, and awake.
Let your eyes open, if they were closed.
Carry this calm focus with you.
You are ready. Begin when you are.
